tasks.description:
**Expected skills:** `karpathy-guidelines`.

Carry the new seam all the way through the live entrypoints by making both direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start consume one shared dependency builder and the same `finalize_launch(...)` boundary. Preserve selected-team propagation and existing error rendering while proving the two entrypoints emit the same audit and preflight outcomes.

## Failure Modes

| Dependency | On error | On timeout | On malformed response |
|------------|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py` composition root | stop and repair wiring there; do not bypass bootstrap with direct adapter imports | N/A | reject partial live dependency wiring by requiring both provider and audit sink |
| `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py` shared builder | stop before runtime start and repair the composition-root wiring there | N/A | reject missing provider or audit sink instead of silently falling back |
| selected-team context from `selected_profile` | preserve team-aware preflight when a team is selected and standalone behavior when it is not | N/A | do not coerce malformed or missing values into a team that changes launch policy unexpectedly |

## Load Profile

- **Shared resources**: sandbox runtime startup plus the shared audit sink file used by both live entrypoints.
- **Per-operation cost**: one shared launch-plan build, one runtime start at most, and one or two audit writes per launch.
- **10x breakpoint**: repeated launch attempts stress audit-write contention and runtime startup before command orchestration itself becomes expensive.

## Negative Tests

- **Malformed inputs**: blank or missing `selected_profile` does not invent a team, and standalone worktree starts still prepare and audit cleanly.
- **Error paths**: direct start and worktree auto-start block before runtime under `locked-down-web` and keep existing user-facing error rendering.
- **Boundary conditions**: both entrypoints emit the same canonical audit-event sequence on success and the same `launch.preflight.failed` record on policy blocks.

## Steps

1. Add `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py` as the single live-launch dependency and plan builder that requires both `agent_provider` and `audit_event_sink`.
2. Wire the default local audit sink through `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py` while preserving the composition-root import boundary.
3. Route direct `scc start` through the shared live-plan builder and `finalize_launch(...)` instead of ad hoc launch wiring.
4. Route worktree auto-start through the same builder and finalizer, preserving `selected_profile` team context so policy-aware preflight does not drift to open-network semantics.
5. Add focused bootstrap, CLI, and integration tests that prove wiring failure is caught early and both live entrypoints stay in parity.

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start both finish live starts through `finalize_launch(...)` instead of ad hoc launch wiring.
- [ ] Worktree auto-start carries `selected_profile` into `StartSessionRequest.team` so provider preflight stays team-aware when a team is active.
- [ ] Focused tests prove direct start and worktree auto-start emit the same audit contract on success and failure.
- [ ] No live entrypoint quietly falls back to open-network semantics because team context or live audit wiring was dropped.
tasks.observabilityImpact:
- Signals added/changed: both live entrypoints append the same canonical preflight and launch events into the durable sink.
- How a future agent inspects this: rerun the focused bootstrap, CLI, and integration tests to localize drift to wiring, direct start, or worktree auto-start.
- Failure state exposed: blocked launches remain visible as `launch.preflight.failed` records before any sandbox is started.
tasks.title: Wire direct start and worktree auto-start through the shared audited launch path

`uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_bootstrap.py ./tests/test_cli.py ./tests/test_integration.py ./tests/test_worktree_cwd.py -q`
`uv run ruff check && uv run mypy src/scc_cli`

# S04: Pre-launch validation and durable audit sink

**Goal:** Add a provider-neutral preflight gate and a durable local launch-audit sink, then route live `scc start` and worktree auto-start through the shared finalize boundary so impossible launches fail before runtime startup and every live launch emits canonical audit events.
**Demo:** After this: TBD

## Tasks
- [ ] **T01: Add provider-neutral preflight evaluation and the durable local audit sink** — **Expected skills:** `karpathy-guidelines`.

Add one provider-neutral launch gate that validates the prepared plan before runtime start and one durable local audit sink behind the same shared launch boundary. Keep the logic anchored to existing launch contracts (`AgentLaunchSpec`, `StartSessionPlan`, effective network policy, and canonical `AuditEvent`) so the slice proves the seam without widening it.

## Failure Modes

| Dependency | On error | On timeout | On malformed response |
|------------|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| `src/scc_cli/application/start_session.py` launch-plan contract | raise a typed `InvalidLaunchPlanError` and stop before runtime start | N/A | reject missing launch metadata, blank provider identity, and blank destination-set names immediately |
| local audit filesystem and lock path | fail closed with `LaunchAuditWriteError` and never start the runtime | surface the existing lock timeout and keep startup blocked | avoid malformed persisted records by serializing canonical `AuditEvent` payloads before write |
| `src/scc_cli/core/errors.py` typed error surface | preserve a specific preflight or audit error instead of generic exceptions | N/A | keep user-facing messages honest about blocked launches and audit failures |

## Load Profile

- **Shared resources**: the audit sink append path used during launch finalization.
- **Per-operation cost**: trivial validation plus up to two audit-event appends per live launch.
- **10x breakpoint**: audit-write contention or sink failures matter before CPU cost does; the boundary must still fail closed.

## Negative Tests

- **Malformed inputs**: missing `agent_launch_spec`, blank provider id, and blank required destination-set names are rejected.
- **Error paths**: a failing audit sink raises `LaunchAuditWriteError`, and a missing live sink raises `LaunchAuditUnavailableError`.
- **Boundary conditions**: open-network launches with empty required destination sets still pass, while `locked-down-web` plus non-empty required destination sets blocks before runtime startup.

## Steps

1. Add provider-neutral preflight validation over `StartSessionPlan.agent_launch_spec` and the effective sandbox network policy.
2. Introduce typed preflight and audit errors that distinguish invalid plans, policy-blocked launches, missing audit wiring, and failed audit writes.
3. Add `finalize_launch(...)` so allowed launches append preflight-passed and launch-started events, while blocked or malformed launches append a canonical preflight-failed event and stop before runtime startup.
4. Add the `AuditEventSink` port and a local append-only JSONL adapter with locking, flush, and fsync semantics.
5. Pin the boundary with focused tests covering allowed launches, blocked launches, malformed plans, sink serialization, and fail-closed audit-write behavior.

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Preflight validation stays provider-neutral and reads only the typed launch plan plus current network policy.
- [ ] Invalid plan data fails with typed `LaunchPreflightError` variants instead of generic runtime errors.
- [ ] Audit persistence is behind the `AuditEventSink` port and implemented as one local append-only JSONL adapter.
- [ ] The shared finalize boundary appends canonical audit events on pass, fail, and start and never starts the runtime after an audit write failure.
  - Estimate: 75m
  - Files: src/scc_cli/application/launch/preflight.py, src/scc_cli/application/launch/finalize_launch.py, src/scc_cli/application/launch/__init__.py, src/scc_cli/application/start_session.py, src/scc_cli/ports/audit_event_sink.py, src/scc_cli/adapters/local_audit_event_sink.py, src/scc_cli/core/errors.py, src/scc_cli/config.py, tests/test_launch_preflight.py, tests/test_local_audit_event_sink.py
  - Verify: `uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_launch_preflight.py ./tests/test_local_audit_event_sink.py -q`
`uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_launch_preflight.py -q -k "allowed_launch or blocks_locked_down_provider_launch_before_runtime_start or fails_closed_when_audit_write_fails"`
- [ ] **T02: Wire direct start and worktree auto-start through the shared audited launch path** — **Expected skills:** `karpathy-guidelines`.

Carry the new seam all the way through the live entrypoints by making both direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start consume one shared dependency builder and the same `finalize_launch(...)` boundary. Preserve selected-team propagation and existing error rendering while proving the two entrypoints emit the same audit and preflight outcomes.

## Failure Modes

| Dependency | On error | On timeout | On malformed response |
|------------|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py` composition root | stop and repair wiring there; do not bypass bootstrap with direct adapter imports | N/A | reject partial live dependency wiring by requiring both provider and audit sink |
| `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py` shared builder | stop before runtime start and repair the composition-root wiring there | N/A | reject missing provider or audit sink instead of silently falling back |
| selected-team context from `selected_profile` | preserve team-aware preflight when a team is selected and standalone behavior when it is not | N/A | do not coerce malformed or missing values into a team that changes launch policy unexpectedly |

## Load Profile

- **Shared resources**: sandbox runtime startup plus the shared audit sink file used by both live entrypoints.
- **Per-operation cost**: one shared launch-plan build, one runtime start at most, and one or two audit writes per launch.
- **10x breakpoint**: repeated launch attempts stress audit-write contention and runtime startup before command orchestration itself becomes expensive.

## Negative Tests

- **Malformed inputs**: blank or missing `selected_profile` does not invent a team, and standalone worktree starts still prepare and audit cleanly.
- **Error paths**: direct start and worktree auto-start block before runtime under `locked-down-web` and keep existing user-facing error rendering.
- **Boundary conditions**: both entrypoints emit the same canonical audit-event sequence on success and the same `launch.preflight.failed` record on policy blocks.

## Steps

1. Add `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py` as the single live-launch dependency and plan builder that requires both `agent_provider` and `audit_event_sink`.
2. Wire the default local audit sink through `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py` while preserving the composition-root import boundary.
3. Route direct `scc start` through the shared live-plan builder and `finalize_launch(...)` instead of ad hoc launch wiring.
4. Route worktree auto-start through the same builder and finalizer, preserving `selected_profile` team context so policy-aware preflight does not drift to open-network semantics.
5. Add focused bootstrap, CLI, and integration tests that prove wiring failure is caught early and both live entrypoints stay in parity.

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start both finish live starts through `finalize_launch(...)` instead of ad hoc launch wiring.
- [ ] Worktree auto-start carries `selected_profile` into `StartSessionRequest.team` so provider preflight stays team-aware when a team is active.
- [ ] Focused tests prove direct start and worktree auto-start emit the same audit contract on success and failure.
- [ ] No live entrypoint quietly falls back to open-network semantics because team context or live audit wiring was dropped.

  - Verify: `uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_bootstrap.py ./tests/test_cli.py ./tests/test_integration.py ./tests/test_worktree_cwd.py -q`
`uv run ruff check && uv run mypy src/scc_cli`
- [ ] **T03: Route dashboard worktree start through the shared audited launch path** — Close the integration loop by making the remaining live worktree auto-start entrypoint in the dashboard consume the same prepared-plan builder and finalize boundary as direct `scc start`. Preserve selected-team context and existing error rendering while proving both live entrypoints now emit the same audit and preflight outcomes.
  - Estimate: 45m
  - Files: src/scc_cli/ui/dashboard/orchestrator.py, src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py, src/scc_cli/application/launch/finalize_launch.py, tests/test_cli.py, tests/test_dashboard_worktree_start.py
  - Verify: uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_cli.py::TestStartCommand::test_start_appends_canonical_launch_audit_events ./tests/test_cli.py::TestStartCommand::test_start_returns_json_error_when_preflight_blocks_runtime ./tests/test_dashboard_worktree_start.py -q
- [ ] **T04: Route direct start and worktree auto-start through the shared launch finalizer** — ## Description

Close the integration loop by forcing both live launch entrypoints to consume the shared builder and to finish through `finalize_launch(...)`. Preserve current sync, dry-run, and output behavior for direct `scc start`, propagate `selected_profile` into worktree auto-start so provider policy stays team-aware, and extend focused CLI/integration coverage so bypassing preflight or audit becomes a failing test instead of silent drift.

## Failure Modes

| Dependency | On error | On timeout | On malformed response |
|------------|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| direct `scc start` entrypoint | surface the preflight error through the existing CLI/JSON boundary and keep runtime stopped | N/A | preserve current human/JSON output contracts rather than swallowing the invalid-plan or blocked-policy reason |
| worktree auto-start team propagation | treat missing `selected_profile` propagation as a correctness bug because preflight would silently fall back to open-network semantics | N/A | preserve direct-start and worktree behavior parity for team-aware launches |
| shared builder plus `finalize_launch(...)` | reuse the same pass/fail boundary for both entrypoints instead of allowing one path to bypass audit or preflight | N/A | keep standalone/offline behavior intact while still reaching the shared boundary |

## Load Profile

- **Shared resources**: shared audit sink, shared dependency builder, fake runtime, and selected-profile config state.
- **Per-operation cost**: one prepared launch plan plus one or two audit appends per live start path.
- **10x breakpoint**: repeated blocked launches will show up as audit noise and failing tests before compute cost becomes interesting.

## Negative Tests

- **Malformed inputs**: standalone/offline combinations preserve current behavior while still flowing through the shared boundary when live startup is requested.
- **Error paths**: blocked preflight keeps the fake runtime at zero launches for both direct start and worktree auto-start.
- **Boundary conditions**: successful standalone starts and successful worktree auto-starts both emit the same canonical event sequence with the correct workspace path and team context.

## Steps

1. Route direct `scc start` through shared live-plan preparation and `finalize_launch(...)`, preserving current sync, dry-run, and JSON/human output behavior.
2. Route worktree auto-start through the same builder and finalizer, deriving team context from `selected_profile` so org-policy preflight and audit behavior match direct start.
3. Extend focused CLI and integration tests proving both entrypoints append canonical events, blocked launches fail before runtime start, and selected-profile propagation is preserved.
4. Finish with the focused entrypoint verification and rely on the slice-level lint/type checks for final confidence.

## Must-Haves

- [ ] `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/flow.py` and `src/scc_cli/commands/worktree/worktree_commands.py` both finish live starts through `finalize_launch(...)`.
- [ ] Worktree auto-start carries `selected_profile` into `StartSessionRequest.team` so provider preflight stays team-aware under locked-down policies.
- [ ] Success paths emit the same canonical audit events regardless of entrypoint.
- [ ] Focused tests fail loudly if either entrypoint bypasses preflight, skips audit writes, or starts the runtime after a blocked preflight.
  - Estimate: 35m
  - Files: src/scc_cli/commands/launch/flow.py, src/scc_cli/commands/worktree/worktree_commands.py, tests/test_cli.py, tests/test_integration.py, tests/test_worktree_cwd.py

- [ ] The shared finalize boundary appends canonical audit events on pass, fail, and start and never starts the runtime after an audit write failure.

# T02: Wire direct start and worktree auto-start through the shared audited launch path

**Expected skills:** `karpathy-guidelines`.

Carry the new seam all the way through the live entrypoints by making both direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start consume one shared dependency builder and the same `finalize_launch(...)` boundary. Preserve selected-team propagation and existing error rendering while proving the two entrypoints emit the same audit and preflight outcomes.

## Failure Modes

| Dependency | On error | On timeout | On malformed response |
|------------|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py` composition root | stop and repair wiring there; do not bypass bootstrap with direct adapter imports | N/A | reject partial live dependency wiring by requiring both provider and audit sink |
| `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py` shared builder | stop before runtime start and repair the composition-root wiring there | N/A | reject missing provider or audit sink instead of silently falling back |
| selected-team context from `selected_profile` | preserve team-aware preflight when a team is selected and standalone behavior when it is not | N/A | do not coerce malformed or missing values into a team that changes launch policy unexpectedly |

## Load Profile

- **Shared resources**: sandbox runtime startup plus the shared audit sink file used by both live entrypoints.
- **Per-operation cost**: one shared launch-plan build, one runtime start at most, and one or two audit writes per launch.
- **10x breakpoint**: repeated launch attempts stress audit-write contention and runtime startup before command orchestration itself becomes expensive.

## Negative Tests

- **Malformed inputs**: blank or missing `selected_profile` does not invent a team, and standalone worktree starts still prepare and audit cleanly.
- **Error paths**: direct start and worktree auto-start block before runtime under `locked-down-web` and keep existing user-facing error rendering.
- **Boundary conditions**: both entrypoints emit the same canonical audit-event sequence on success and the same `launch.preflight.failed` record on policy blocks.

## Steps

1. Add `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py` as the single live-launch dependency and plan builder that requires both `agent_provider` and `audit_event_sink`.
2. Wire the default local audit sink through `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py` while preserving the composition-root import boundary.
3. Route direct `scc start` through the shared live-plan builder and `finalize_launch(...)` instead of ad hoc launch wiring.
4. Route worktree auto-start through the same builder and finalizer, preserving `selected_profile` team context so policy-aware preflight does not drift to open-network semantics.
5. Add focused bootstrap, CLI, and integration tests that prove wiring failure is caught early and both live entrypoints stay in parity.

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start both finish live starts through `finalize_launch(...)` instead of ad hoc launch wiring.
- [ ] Worktree auto-start carries `selected_profile` into `StartSessionRequest.team` so provider preflight stays team-aware when a team is active.
- [ ] Focused tests prove direct start and worktree auto-start emit the same audit contract on success and failure.
- [ ] No live entrypoint quietly falls back to open-network semantics because team context or live audit wiring was dropped.

## Inputs

- ``src/scc_cli/application/launch/preflight.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/application/launch/finalize_launch.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/ports/audit_event_sink.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/adapters/local_audit_event_sink.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/commands/launch/flow.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/commands/worktree/worktree_commands.py``
- ``tests/test_bootstrap.py``
- ``tests/test_cli.py``
- ``tests/test_integration.py``

## Expected Output

- ``src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/commands/launch/flow.py``
- ``src/scc_cli/commands/worktree/worktree_commands.py``
- ``tests/fakes/__init__.py``
- ``tests/conftest.py``
- ``tests/test_bootstrap.py``
- ``tests/test_cli.py``
- ``tests/test_integration.py``
- ``tests/test_worktree_cwd.py``

## Verification

`uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_bootstrap.py ./tests/test_cli.py ./tests/test_integration.py ./tests/test_worktree_cwd.py -q`
`uv run ruff check && uv run mypy src/scc_cli`

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: both live entrypoints append the same canonical preflight and launch events into the durable sink.
- How a future agent inspects this: rerun the focused bootstrap, CLI, and integration tests to localize drift to wiring, direct start, or worktree auto-start.
- Failure state exposed: blocked launches remain visible as `launch.preflight.failed` records before any sandbox is started.

arguments.content:
# S04: Pre-launch validation and durable audit sink

**Goal:** Add a provider-neutral preflight gate and a durable local launch-audit sink, then route live `scc start` and worktree auto-start through the shared finalize boundary so impossible launches fail before runtime startup and every live launch emits canonical audit events.
**Demo:** After this: direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start both block malformed or `locked-down-web` provider launches before runtime startup and append canonical `launch.preflight.passed`, `launch.preflight.failed`, and `launch.started` events to SCC’s local launch-audit JSONL sink.

## Must-Haves

- Provider-neutral preflight validates `AgentLaunchSpec` metadata and blocks provider-required destination sets under incompatible network policy before runtime startup.
- SCC persists canonical `AuditEvent` records through one local append-only JSONL sink behind an `AuditEventSink` port and fails closed on sink write failures.
- Direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start both reuse the shared live dependency builder and `finalize_launch(...)`, with selected-team context preserved where policy depends on it.
- Focused tests prove allowed launches, blocked launches, malformed plans, sink failures, and entrypoint parity.

## Threat Surface

- **Abuse**: bypassing provider policy checks by starting through a drifted entrypoint; malformed provider launch metadata; forcing a locked-down team launch to behave like open network by dropping team context.
- **Data exposure**: launch audit records include provider, workspace, team, network-policy, and sandbox identifiers; they must not include secrets, raw settings payloads, or tokens.
- **Input trust**: `selected_profile`, org-config-derived network policy, provider-owned `required_destination_sets`, workspace path, and the configured audit sink path all cross the trust boundary into launch orchestration.

## Requirement Impact

- **Requirements touched**: `R001`
- **Re-verify**: launch preflight contract tests, local sink tests, bootstrap wiring, direct-start JSON/human error behavior, worktree auto-start parity, and lint/type gates.
- **Decisions revisited**: `D003`, `D004`, `D006`

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: integration
- Real runtime required: no
- Human/UAT required: no

## Verification

- `uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_launch_preflight.py ./tests/test_local_audit_event_sink.py ./tests/test_bootstrap.py ./tests/test_cli.py ./tests/test_integration.py ./tests/test_worktree_cwd.py -q`
- `uv run ruff check && uv run mypy src/scc_cli`
- `uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" --tb=short -q`

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: canonical `launch.preflight.passed`, `launch.preflight.failed`, and `launch.started` audit events persisted to the durable sink.
- Inspection surfaces: focused launch/audit test files plus the on-disk sink at `~/.config/scc/audit/launch-events.jsonl`.
- Failure visibility: provider id, network policy, required destination sets, failure reason, sandbox id, and audit destination path surface before or alongside runtime start.
- Redaction constraints: persist provider/workspace/team metadata only; never store raw settings payloads, tokens, or secrets.

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: `src/scc_cli/application/start_session.py`, `src/scc_cli/core/contracts.py`, `src/scc_cli/core/errors.py`, `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py`, `src/scc_cli/adapters/claude_agent_provider.py`, `src/scc_cli/adapters/codex_agent_provider.py`
- New wiring introduced in this slice: `src/scc_cli/application/launch/preflight.py`, `src/scc_cli/application/launch/finalize_launch.py`, `src/scc_cli/ports/audit_event_sink.py`, `src/scc_cli/adapters/local_audit_event_sink.py`, and the shared live builder in `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py`
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: bounded operator-facing audit inspection and further launch/support decomposition remain for S05.

## Tasks

- [ ] **T01: Add provider-neutral preflight evaluation and the durable local audit sink** `est:75m`
  - Why: This closes the core boundary contract for S04: malformed or policy-blocked launches must fail before runtime startup, and every decision must have one durable canonical audit record.
  - Files: `src/scc_cli/application/launch/preflight.py`, `src/scc_cli/application/launch/finalize_launch.py`, `src/scc_cli/application/start_session.py`, `src/scc_cli/ports/audit_event_sink.py`, `src/scc_cli/adapters/local_audit_event_sink.py`, `src/scc_cli/core/errors.py`, `src/scc_cli/config.py`, `tests/test_launch_preflight.py`, `tests/test_local_audit_event_sink.py`
  - Do: Add provider-neutral preflight validation over `StartSessionPlan.agent_launch_spec` and the effective sandbox network policy; introduce typed preflight/audit errors; add the `AuditEventSink` port plus one local append-only JSONL adapter with locking, flush, and fsync semantics; and gate `finalize_launch(...)` so allowed launches append pass/start events while blocked or malformed launches append a canonical failure event and stop before runtime startup.
  - Verify: `uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_launch_preflight.py ./tests/test_local_audit_event_sink.py -q`
  - Done when: focused tests prove malformed plans fail early, `locked-down-web` plus non-empty required destination sets block before runtime start, open-network launches with empty required destination sets still pass, and audit write failures fail closed.
- [ ] **T02: Wire direct start and worktree auto-start through the shared audited launch path** `est:75m`
  - Why: The preflight and sink only stay honest if the real entrypoints reuse one builder and one final boundary instead of carrying drifted inline wiring.
  - Files: `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py`, `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py`, `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/flow.py`, `src/scc_cli/commands/worktree/worktree_commands.py`, `tests/fakes/__init__.py`, `tests/conftest.py`, `tests/test_bootstrap.py`, `tests/test_cli.py`, `tests/test_integration.py`, `tests/test_worktree_cwd.py`
  - Do: Add one shared live dependency/plan builder that requires both `agent_provider` and `audit_event_sink`; wire the default local audit sink through `bootstrap.py`; route direct `scc start` and worktree auto-start through the shared builder and `finalize_launch(...)`; and preserve `selected_profile` propagation so team-aware policy preflight does not drift back to open-network semantics.
  - Verify: `uv run pytest --rootdir "$PWD" ./tests/test_bootstrap.py ./tests/test_cli.py ./tests/test_integration.py ./tests/test_worktree_cwd.py -q` and `uv run ruff check && uv run mypy src/scc_cli`
  - Done when: both entrypoints emit the same canonical audit-event sequence on success, blocked launches stop before runtime start, missing provider/audit wiring fails early, and focused parity tests localize any future drift.

## Files Likely Touched

- `src/scc_cli/application/launch/preflight.py`
- `src/scc_cli/application/launch/finalize_launch.py`
- `src/scc_cli/ports/audit_event_sink.py`
- `src/scc_cli/adapters/local_audit_event_sink.py`
- `src/scc_cli/bootstrap.py`
- `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/dependencies.py`
- `src/scc_cli/commands/launch/flow.py`
- `src/scc_cli/commands/worktree/worktree_commands.py`
- `tests/test_launch_preflight.py`
- `tests/test_local_audit_event_sink.py`
- `tests/test_bootstrap.py`
- `tests/test_cli.py`
- `tests/test_integration.py`
- `tests/test_worktree_cwd.py`

# T01: Add provider-neutral preflight evaluation and the durable local audit sink

**Expected skills:** `karpathy-guidelines`.

Add one provider-neutral launch gate that validates the prepared plan before runtime start and one durable local audit sink behind the same shared launch boundary. Keep the logic anchored to existing launch contracts (`AgentLaunchSpec`, `StartSessionPlan`, effective network policy, and canonical `AuditEvent`) so the slice proves the seam without widening it.

## Failure Modes

| Dependency | On error | On timeout | On malformed response |
|------------|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| `src/scc_cli/application/start_session.py` launch-plan contract | raise a typed `InvalidLaunchPlanError` and stop before runtime start | N/A | reject missing launch metadata, blank provider identity, and blank destination-set names immediately |
| local audit filesystem and lock path | fail closed with `LaunchAuditWriteError` and never start the runtime | surface the existing lock timeout and keep startup blocked | avoid malformed persisted records by serializing canonical `AuditEvent` payloads before write |
| `src/scc_cli/core/errors.py` typed error surface | preserve a specific preflight or audit error instead of generic exceptions | N/A | keep user-facing messages honest about blocked launches and audit failures |
